commentaries.author: John Chrysostom
commentaries.content: You see that he used these apologies, as writing to persons grieved with him, as to those who turned away, who were disposed as towards a transgressor, not enduring even to hear his name. Inasmuch then as he asked from those who hated him what all others ask from those who love them [their prayers for him], therefore he here introduces this; saying, We trust that we have a good conscience. For do not tell me of accusations; our conscience, he says, in nothing hurts us; nor are we conscious to ourselves that we have plotted against you. For we trust, he says, that we have a good conscience among all, not among the Gentiles only, but also among you. We have done nothing with deceitfulness, nothing with hypocrisy: for it was probable that these [calumnies] were reported respecting him. For they have been informed concerning you (it is said) that you teach apostasy. Acts 21:21 Not as an enemy, he means, nor as an adversary I write these things, but as a friend. And this he shows also by what follows.

methodology.step_1: Parse main page with BeautifulSoup for structure
methodology.step_3: Use Firecrawl to get full content from 'Go to Commentary' links

advantages: Records verses even without commentaries for complete tracking
